item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Apologies for late response)

It's a meta joke. Apparently all the SCPs prior to this one that had the number 048 were very low-quality and ended up getting deleted.

item.title: Re: Rename 682
item.content:encoded:
Well, this scenario was mentioned in 682's termination logs.

Item: Reclassification to SCP-048

Tissue Test Record: N/A

Termination Log: Denied by O5 Command

Note: While it could potentially give us the result we desire, the possibility of SCP-682 being stolen instead of being destroyed after its reclassification could cause more problems than waiting longer to find an alternative solution to the issue at hand, not to mention the fact that the possibility of losing everyone working with SCP-682 would be a major catastrophe to the foundation. - O5-7

Additionally that assumes the higher-ups are down with officially recognizing 048 as a real phenomenon and not just a workplace superstition.

item.title: Rename 682
item.content:encoded: Why don’t you just rename 682 to 048

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: C U R S E D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with Nyctibius, it sitting on the fence of being a joke SCP or keep it in the list it gave me a chortle. =)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can upvote now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Partially a joke SCP. I honestly don’t know if I really like this SCP (if I can even call it that) but I’ll just +1 for article being unique.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely done sir. This article is very nice. Although, wouldn't SCP 013 be a unluckier one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Uh… no?

This is sitting at a very /comfy/ 800+ upvotes showing that many people have a positive view of this article. Not only that, but there isn't much you can do with a concept like this where said SCP can not exist so I think, and many others may share my view, this is find as is.

What would you have in mind for a rewrite of this article if it were to somehow happen?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It really needs a rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A unique idea, and an interesting concept to be made (or in this case, not made) an SCP.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's kinda wired how many people don't believe it. they deal with "supernatural" things on a daily basis. and there's statistical evidence to back it up

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ǝɔuǝɟɟo ou 'ǝlɔᴉʇɹɐ ǝɥʇ pɐǝɹ 'slǝqɐl ʇᴉ ʇɐɥʍ ɟo sǝsodsᴉp ʇɐɥʇ ɹǝqɯnu ԀƆS uɐ sᴉ ʇI

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some Crazy Paradox-048

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't care if it's necro. I want to thank you for pointing me at the word "exegesis".

*Saved to brain*

item.title: that cool idea everybody had
item.content:encoded:
it'd escape, whatever, it does that all the time. the real reason not to renumber 682 to 048: please do the math on "a 50% higher rate of turnover due to death, dismemberment, and disciplinary action" as it would apply to 682 containment

also, 682 escaping wouldn't fulfill the curse. the curse causes the items to get delisted through the Foundation's normal processes; 682 is too dangerous and too conspicuous for the Foundation to ever delist it for some minor reason like "we don't know where it is right now". even if 682 was somehow neutralized, they still wouldn't delist it - what if it stops being dead? it's known for being pretty good at that, after all…

not sure anything short of destroying the Foundation completely would pry ol' 682 out of the database, tbh. is the curse strong enough to cause an XK or something? if you hand it 682 on a silver platter, yeah, it probably is. so that's another reason.1

it's actually really funny to me that this item collected so many suggestions for a 682 crosstest2 - the impulse to try and rub 682 up against every single mainlist item to see if something "funny" happens is a very oldschool/lolfoundation/SCP-048 kind of idea. i used to fall for it myself - a long time back i got scared off the site for a while because i was34 called out for suggesting a 682/963 crosstest in the chat. (the quality of that tale5 is entirely unrelated to the quality of my suggestion6)

i want to go back to that 50% statistic for a second - to me that detail is honestly the most lolfoundation aspect of the whole article. the foundation - constantly scouring and analyzing and collating demographic data from everywhere so they'll know right away if a rural county in Minnesota suddenly has twice as many Freds in it, but they're writing off a 50% increase in employee turnover as a coincidence? is this the low-clearance complete lie version of a keter infohazard or something?

anyway that's far more analysis than the idea probably deserves, sorry about that, thank you for coming to my ted talk

Footnotes
1. if i had my way with this article i'd want to add some kind of note from an anomalous causality researcher strongly urging that 048 not ever be assigned to any item that's Too Big To Lose (and possibly add some kind of hint that the "vampyre boat" incident may have been a sign that this so-called None-class anomaly is actively attempting to breach containment)
2. i'm assuming all the examples on the last few pages were jokes about the examples on the first few pages
3. rightly
4. but rudely
5. which didn't exist at the time
6. which was bad

item.title: east asian belief
item.content:encoded:
is actually related to the fact that four has the same pronunciation as death… or at least very similar to.

Apologies for necroposting

item.title: Re: Wait
item.content:encoded: It'd just escape or something. SCP-682 can't be destroyed. That's the whole point.

item.title: Wait
item.content:encoded: Why not re-designate SCP-682 to this? ;)

item.title: Re: SCP-048 extended testing log
item.content:encoded: …No. The idea doesn't need expansion, just make the researcher less comically unprofessional, and get rid of that last addendum.

item.title: SCP-048 extended testing log
item.content:encoded:
An extended testing log of bad ideas?

3/14/10— SCP-048 designation assigned to a coffee cup that kills anyone who touches it.
3/27/10 SCP-048 misplaced in Cafeteria. Two researchers dead, object's location unknown

4/13/10 SCP-048 designation assigned to a Dragon-like monster.
4/26/10 SCP-048 discovered to be a Komodo Dragon. Dr. Woodwall bitten.

6/16/10 SCP-048 designation assigned to time-traveling fire hydrant.
6/03/10 SCP-048 discovered to be non-anomalous. Typo resulted in belief that object had time-traveled

6/17/10 SCP-048 designation assigned to SCP-682, but worse.
6/30/10 SCP-048 discovered to be SCP-682 in a scary mask. Scary mask removed, and SCP-682 returned to SCP-682 designation.

7/07/10 SCP-048 designation assigned to SPC-048.
7/20/10 Dusty Shark (Carcharhinus obscurus) now endangered due to fistcuffs. SPC-048 designation returned to SCP-048.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the concept, and the article as-is holds up very well, but at the same time I feel like it needs a minor touch up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i feel like it would benefit from having some of those added in an experiment log or even attempts to destroy other SCP's by re-numbering them

The Foundation does not destroy SCP objects. Of course, there are exceptions, like 682, but most of the stuff here is to be contained.

However, something similar to what you suggest here was actually used for Document 050 before.

+ Spoilers
- Spoilers

Check the revision history of SCP-048, and view revision 7. The description of this object had a link to Dr. Bright's personnel file for around a day.

Now, Document 050 was rewritten on revision 404, but you can view revision 403. The first entry of this prank war describes how Dr. English listed Dr. Bright as SCP-048.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels like an awesome concept and skimming the discussion looks like it was spawned by an equally terrible list of bad SCP's. i feel like it would benefit from having some of those added in an experiment log or even attempts to destroy other SCP's by re-numbering them. right now it sounds more like a joke than an SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this scp, and think it's fun, but honestly the fact that its an ancient in-joke about badly written scps that no one can actually read now makes me feel sort of left out? What I'm saying is, I feel like it'd basically include more people in on the joke if the bad 048s could be read somewhere. (I really wanna read the dumb vampire boat okay)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What new addendum?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the new addendum lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shut up with your mind games!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, SCP-048 is the slot in the database that the desription of the slot is held in. The description describes the slot, while being held within the slot. It's slots all the way down!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So in a way SCP-048 is the SCP number "048"? That ought to be confusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God damn I really want this to be better.

The idea of "a cursed entry on the database" is fucking great but the tone is so lolfoundation-ey and the exploration of the idea is really weak. Blender accident, O5s commenting about fanfiction, ughhhh this concept deserves better writing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't this thread become lost as it is also under the curse of being 048?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure exactly what you mean by this, but given the ways that the SCP can be "lost", it's possible it could just end up being not contained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I expected this to be SCP-13. I wonder if there's a good tale in how this became cursed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems like quite a cop-out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't have a better description, but I do remember some details.

Such as it saying that the vampires faze through walls and " have a habit of being uncontained ".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The classic "Why is this not an -J?!"-SCP.
The horrible blender accident is indeed worth a laugh.

item.title: Staff Post - Page Lock Notice
item.content:encoded: And, given that this is not the first time staff has needed to revert an unauthorized edit on this article, the page is now locked. Anyone wishing to make any further edits to the article should obtain author and/or staff permission first.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Big_Train_52, instead of replying to your own comments, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean SCPs, not CAPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can you resend number other CAPs? Why not remember 678

item.title: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Noting that the last two versions of this article have been reverted. There is absolutely no reason to blackbox the O5 designation.

item.title: Re: Why is this SCP still a thing?
item.content:encoded: It makes more sense in-universe. Out-of-universe such behavior is a natural consequence of the way SCP numbers are chosen, the deletions policy, and Sturgeon's Law. It is completely expected and wholly explicable.

item.title: Re: Why is this SCP still a thing?
item.content:encoded:
I think it makes sense in-universe as well. Something higher up in the cosmos of the universe has a sense of humor, and it revolves around the number 48. The Foundation's 48, that is.

Or maybe it's just pure coincidence, but you know what they say. First time's an accident, second time's a coincidence, third time's bad luck, and fourth time and on something is fucking with you.

item.title: Re: Why is this SCP still a thing?
item.content:encoded:
Well, A. because as of now it has a rating of +355
B. Because it does mostly make sense in universe
C. It's a fun in-joke and relic for people who were around to see all the god-awful scips that passed through this slot.

item.title: Why is this SCP still a thing?
item.content:encoded: I'm sure SCP-048 was funny back in 2009 when DrClef wrote this article in response to the high number of bad SCP articles that were written to this slot, but it's been 2,539 days since then and the joke isn't particularly relevant anymore. Sure, you could say that to get some of the context for it, you could look at the lowest rated articles, but most of the lolFoundation articles were removed. This really should have been a -J from the start. (I'm aware that I'm in the minority on this and the power of a +355 from it's long history on the site isn't going to be counteracted anytime soon.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, atleast, I don't think it does. The SCP is somewhat of a Joke SCP.

item.title: And now...
item.content:encoded: And now we have SCP-2134 instead. Seriously, that thing has been deleted about 8 times!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this is the reason why 048 is what it is, but I think a throwback tale based on the idea of designating Clef as 048 would be pretty amusing to read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wonder if anyone has kept track of deletions that closely. I would be surprised. You might be able to get a lot of them via the wayback machine, though.

EDIT: Nope, it looks like the oldest version there is still the cursed number one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seriously? That's hilarious. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, it would have been hilarious if they gave Clef the number when they considered designating him an SCP.

I'm pretty sure that the "cursed number" bit comes from the IRL fact that articles posted to this slot were constantly deleted because they were low-rated, not an actual in-universe plot thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would love a list of things given the number SCP-048. Actually, it would have been hilarious if they gave Clef the number when they considered designating him an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We could've used Scp 048 to destroy Scp 682 or 1048-A through C. The possibilities! :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one, and its quite amusing. It's really interesting, and addendum 2 is hilarious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Historical joke nobody will get:
I guess it's not the only time Article 48 caused death and misery with its mere use.

item.title: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Versions 14 and 15 of this article were reverted, as they constituted significant changes to the article without author permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So delightfully meta. +1

item.title: Re: So, I'm gonna guess what happened here...
item.content:encoded:
and in the end clef (or gears) was like "Screw it" and they made something up about a curse SCP number?

Original author is Clef; you can check the original author by clicking the "History" link at the bottom of the corresponding page.

Clef is also active on the site; you can ask him questions in PM.

item.title: Re: So, I'm gonna guess what happened here...
item.content:encoded:
Really, now, are you saying anything other than, "But I want a low number!"

If not, then would you be happy if a popular Series II SCP, like 1437 or 1981, was placed in 048?

And if that answer is yes, then why bother?
